diagrams.title: Four Colour Example Map
diagrams.description: A typical map showing how four colours suffice to colour adjacent regions
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates practical application of Four Colour Theorem

diagrams.title: Euler's Formula Visualization
diagrams.description: A planar graph with V=4, E=6, F=4 showing V-E+F=2
diagrams.purpose: Visual proof of Euler's formula for planar graphs

diagrams.title: Dual Graph Construction
diagrams.description: Shows how regions in a map transform to vertices in dual graph
diagrams.purpose: Understanding reduction of map colouring to graph colouring

diagrams.title: Heawood Graph on Torus
diagrams.description: The Heawood graph embedded on a torus requiring 6 colours
diagrams.purpose: Shows colourability differences on non-planar surfaces
